patch.value:
## Root cause (reproduced 2026-06-14)
The prior pm-1byt fix addressed an adjacent timer but the bug persists whenever `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT` (or the base endpoint) is set and unreachable — which is the real dev/prod condition that produced the original exit-13 report.

`finishTelemetryCommand` (src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:1710) fire-and-forgets the OTEL span export: `void exportLocalOtelSpan(...).catch(()=>{})`. The fetch uses `AbortSignal.timeout(5000)` whose timer is unref'd, but the in-flight connecting socket keeps libuv alive. So although runPmCli resolves quickly, the process cannot exit until the fetch settles.

### Reproduction (sandbox)
- telemetry on + `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT=http://192.0.2.1:4318/v1/traces` (blackhole)
- `pm get <id>` (read) AND `pm update <id> --priority high` (mutation) BOTH take ~10.7s, vs 143-165ms with `PM_TELEMETRY_OTEL_DISABLED=1` or `PM_NO_TELEMETRY=1`.
- Confirms the OTEL trace export is the sole culprit; affects every command, not just mutations.

In an agent's chained `cmd && cmd && cmd` audit flow this lingering/abort interacts with the top-level await in dist/cli.js and produces the exit-13 / partial-chain behaviour reported in GH-209.

## Fix
Route OTEL span export off the parent's critical path into the already-detached, unref'd telemetry flush worker (same model as the event queue): build the OTLP span payload (pure, no network) and persist it to a bounded pending-spans store; the detached flush worker drains + POSTs it. Parent does zero telemetry network I/O -> exits promptly and deterministically. Preserve inline behaviour for tests (shouldFlushInline).

## Acceptance criteria
- With an unreachable traces endpoint, read + mutation commands return in <500ms (no ~10s linger, no exit 13).
- OTEL spans are still delivered when the endpoint is reachable (via the worker).
- Pending-spans store is bounded (max entries + TTL/retention), atomic-write safe.
- Full coverage gate green.

Follow-up to [[pm-1byt]]. Related: GH-206 (test-all parent staying alive is the same lingering-fetch symptom).
GH-209 follow-up: OTEL span export keeps the CLI alive ~10s (and can exit 13) when the traces endpoint is unreachable

value.author: claude-code-agent
value.text: Root cause confirmed + core fix landed on branch fix/telemetry-otel-export-nonblocking-health-diagnostics. Reproduced: telemetry on + unreachable OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_ENDPOINT made EVERY command (read+mutation) linger ~10.7s (fire-and-forget exportLocalOtelSpan keeping the parent event loop alive on a connecting socket). Fix: refactored exportLocalOtelSpan -> pure buildOtelSpanRequest (no network); finishTelemetryCommand now persists the built OTLP span to a bounded pending-spans queue (runtime/telemetry/otel-spans.jsonl, cap 500, TTL/attempts pruning) and the detached unref'd flush worker POSTs it via new flushPendingOtelSpans, wired through flushTelemetryArtifacts (worker + inline paths). Parent does ZERO telemetry network -> verified 121ms read / 154-169ms mutation with both unreachable AND reachable endpoints; spans delivered + drained by worker (mock OTLP got 2 spans, file emptied). Runtime state now records last_otel_attempt/success/failure + pending_otel_spans.

author: claude-code-agent
message: Scrub private-IP literal from reproduction notes (use RFC 5737 TEST-NET); satisfies security:scan
